title: Cyanogen
content:
{{Top|leg|notoc=1}}
[[File:Cyanogen.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Cyanogen gas released from Ooroo's tank during the Battle of Kirrek]]
'''Cyanogen''' was a gas that was poisonous to [[Human/Legends|humans]] and some other [[Species/Legends|species]]. However, a few species needed it for survival, in particular the [[Celegian/Legends|Celegians]]. Other species, such as [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshans]], [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]], and [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustans]] were immune to its toxic effects.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>

It was an important factor in the defeat of [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] forces on [[Kirrek]] during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. The Celegian [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ooroo/Legends|Ooroo]] sacrificed himself by tipping his chamber filled with cyanogen into the Massassi and, as it shattered, it killed many warriors. He was subsequently poisoned by [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]].

Around [[3951 BBY]] the [[Jekk'Jekk Tarr]] [[Cantina/Legends|Cantina]] on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] was oriented towards clientele from species that could breathe the gas, and circulated cyanogen as a deterrent against humans entering the bar. The [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Mira]] was able to enter using an [[Environment suit/Legends|enviro-suit]] that she had stolen from [[Meetra Surik]]. This forced Surik to enter without protection to rescue Mira. She was temporarily subdued by the gas but overcame it due to her [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]] and [[The Force/Legends|Force]] abilities.

Cyanogen was a constituent of [[cyanogen silicate]].<ref name="LTSP">''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Paragon]]''</ref>
